audit_summary.overall_assessment: struct.json v2_2 is now executable for the active relation-graph path: pipeline_entrypoints, artifact_manifest_contract, and validator_registry are connected to scripts/vlm/run_struct_pipeline.py. The v18 relation-graph smoke and locked paths can be run with one command and emit manifests plus runner/validator audits. Remaining weaknesses are narrower: graph-reconstruction submodules, model registry paths, candidate-source registry, gate levels, and review-pack contracts still need more executable detail.
what_is_now_good: Developer experience is explicitly protected: logical decoupling is not allowed to become physical fragmentation.
The graph reconstruction bottleneck is correctly first-class, not hidden as a cap/reranker tweak.
Symbol objectness, instance split, and type classification are structurally separated.
Relation taxonomy now maps candidate relations to final scene-graph relations.
Feature leakage and offline-only labels are explicitly called out.
audit_summary.highest_priority_remaining_change: Continue turning the remaining architecture sections into executable contracts without fragmenting developer workflows: graph-reconstruction submodule status, candidate-source registry, gate levels, and review-pack schema are next.

implemented_since_previous_audit.remaining_gap: DetectorCandidate validates current v18 candidate_stream shape but still warns that legacy candidates lack provenance/audit_trace. SceneGraph and FeatureLeakage remain planned lightweight validators.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: module_contracts now exist with producers, consumers, required fields, and validation notes.
resolved_from_previous_audit.remaining_gap: Most validations are still natural-language strings, not reusable validators with command paths and pass/fail artifact outputs.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: relation_aware_compression was reframed as graph_reconstruction with explicit submodules.
resolved_from_previous_audit.remaining_gap: No graph-reconstruction command bundle or artifact manifest is defined for the active v18 scripts.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: candidate_frontend now lists objectness, panoptic/segment, primitive, canonicalizer, and budgeter submodules.
resolved_from_previous_audit.remaining_gap: Those submodules are not yet mapped to concrete current scripts or future implementation status.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: symbol_instance_and_type_path was added as a separate stage from legacy symbol_fixture.
relation_taxonomy now maps bounded_by, contains_symbol, labeled_by_text, and adjacent_to to canonical final relations.
feature_sources now separates inference_available, offline_only, and derived_from_inference_available.
resolved_from_previous_audit.remaining_gap: No validator exists yet to scan actual artifact fields against feature_sources.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: model_registry now lists experts and relation_graph_policy_v18.
resolved_from_previous_audit.remaining_gap: Several registry entries still lack concrete checkpoint paths, training dataset paths, eval report paths, and calibration report paths.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: uncertainty_contract now declares abstain/review fields and triggers.
runtime_budgets now define candidate, edge, graph-reduction, invalid-graph, and latency/resource targets.
resolved_from_previous_audit.remaining_gap: Budgets are not yet connected to a manifest or CI-style gate.

resolved_from_previous_audit.resolution: evaluation_protocol now defines split policy, promotion gates, required reports, and known bad proxy metrics.

new_structural_findings.finding: developer_experience_policy requires smoke_pipeline and locked_pipeline. This is now implemented for the active v18 relation-graph path through scripts/vlm/run_struct_pipeline.py.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: The project can still drift into manual multi-command workflows for the most important current work: relation_graph_reconstruction_v18/listwise policy. That undermines both developer convenience and reproducibility.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Keep using the one-command runner for relation_graph_v18_smoke and relation_graph_v18_locked; extend the same pattern to full_raster_moe_v18_locked when the downstream modules are ready.

new_structural_findings.finding: The file now defines artifact_manifest_contract and the runner emits manifests for smoke and locked relation-graph runs.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: Without a manifest schema, every pipeline will keep inventing report paths. Developers will still need to search scripts and todo.json to understand what a run produced.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Keep manifest generation mandatory for new aggregated entrypoints; avoid duplicating volatile metric snapshots outside eval/audit artifacts.

new_structural_findings.finding: validator_registry exists and scripts/vlm/run_struct_pipeline.py now dispatches DetectorCandidate, RelationEdgeCandidate, SelectedRelationEdge, and SourceIntegrity checks into reports/vlm/struct_validator_registry_audit.json.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: Natural-language validators are useful documentation, but they cannot be run in CI or reused by scripts. This keeps contract drift likely.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Promote SceneGraph and FeatureLeakage from planned to lightweight executable validators; tighten DetectorCandidate warnings once v18 candidate_stream emits provenance/audit_trace.

new_structural_findings.finding: graph_reconstruction is structurally strong, but its submodules are not connected to current files, missing status, or planned files.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: It is hard to tell what is already implemented, what is planned, and what is still conceptual. That hurts prioritization.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: For every graph_reconstruction submodule, add implementation_status, current_owner_files, expected_artifacts, and next_missing_piece. For example wall_segment_duplicate_collapse should be status=planned_next with owner scripts/vlm/relation_graph_reconstruction_v18.py.

new_structural_findings.finding: model_registry is useful but uneven: relation_graph_policy_v18 has concrete paths, while most expert entries only have prose summaries.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: A replacement contract is only actionable if the registry points to actual datasets, checkpoints, eval reports, and calibration reports.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Add optional nullable fields for checkpoint, training_dataset, eval_report, calibration_report, and status. Use null with reason when a path is unknown or not applicable, rather than prose-only summaries.

new_structural_findings.finding: candidate_frontend lists desired submodules, but does not map current proposal sources to those submodules.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: Developers cannot quickly see whether a candidate came from primitive recall, Hough wall, fallback room, patch heatmap, or room-wall repair.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Add candidate_source_registry mapping proposal_source values to submodule, family, expected payload fields, known failure modes, and audit counters.

new_structural_findings.finding: evaluation_protocol promotion gates are production-grade, but the file does not define lighter development gates for quick iteration.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: A 0.98 production target is correct, but developers need smoke-level and regression-level gates that fail fast without requiring a full locked run.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Add gate_levels: smoke, dev_regression, locked_candidate, production_promotion. Each level should define allowed runtime, sample scope, blocking metrics, and whether failure blocks merge/adoption.

new_structural_findings.finding: The architecture has schema_version globally and DetectorCandidate has a contract version, but other object types have no per-object schema version.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: RelationEdgeCandidate and SceneGraph are likely to evolve. Without per-object versions, old artifacts become ambiguous.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Add schema_version to every module_contract object. Require readers to accept current and one previous minor version or fail with a clear migration error.

new_structural_findings.finding: review_and_export says visual overlays/failure gallery, but the architecture does not define what a review pack must contain.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: Review packs can become inconsistent across modules, making human diagnosis slower.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Add review_pack_contract with overlay image paths, top failure buckets, sampled false positives/false negatives, source artifact links, and per-module trace summaries.

new_structural_findings.finding: struct.json now overlaps with todo.json and README on metrics and current status.
new_structural_findings.why_it_matters: Status values can go stale quickly in multiple places.
new_structural_findings.recommended_change: Keep struct.json as mostly stable architecture and contract. Move volatile metrics into artifact manifests and reports, and keep only references plus latest-known snapshot fields where necessary.

recommended_next_struct_changes: Normalize model_registry entries to use explicit path fields with null plus reason when unavailable.
Add candidate_source_registry for primitive, Hough, fallback room, patch heatmap, room-wall repair, and future panoptic sources.
Add per-object schema_version to all module_contracts.
Promote SceneGraph and FeatureLeakage validator_registry entries from planned to executable lightweight scans.
Reduce volatile metric duplication in struct.json once artifact manifests exist.

reference_patterns_to_keep.why: This is the right developer-experience pattern: many modules internally, one command for normal execution.
Dataset builders already use manifests; pipeline runs should use the same idea.
Keep detailed audits as separate artifacts, but collect them under one run manifest.
